file_summary: This file contains unit tests for the Serializer class in the itsdangerous library. It tests serialization/deserialization functionality, error handling, configuration options, and edge cases. The tests use pytest fixtures and parameterization to cover various scenarios including valid serialization, tampered data, different signer configurations, and file operations.

line_windows.summary: This window covers the initial imports, the coerce_str helper function, and the beginning of the TestSerializer class. It includes: imports for testing and serialization; overload definitions and implementation of coerce_str (converts strings to bytes if reference is bytes); TestSerializer class definition with pytest fixtures for serializer creation; and the first test method test_serializer that verifies basic serialization/deserialization round-trip for various data types.

This window covers the middle section of TestSerializer tests. It includes: test_changed_value with parameterized transformations to verify tampered data raises BadSignature; test_bad_signature_exception for truncated signatures; test_bad_payload_exception for corrupted payloads; test_loads_unsafe testing unsafe loading with various error conditions; test_file for file-based serialization operations; and test_alt_salt for testing salt parameter validation.

This window covers the latter part of TestSerializer and the standalone test_digests function. It includes: test_signer_cls for custom signer classes; test_signer_kwargs for signer configuration; test_serializer_kwargs for serializer configuration; test_fallback_signers for multiple signer support; test_iter_unsigners for iterating through fallback signers; and the standalone test_digests function that verifies specific digest method outputs produce expected signature strings.
